name: Helena's Hawaiian Food
tagline: James Beard Award winner in a Kalihi strip mall — the pipikaula short ribs will ruin you for all other beef.
mustOrder: Hawaiian plate (pipikaula short ribs, lomi salmon, poi, haupia)
parking: Strip mall parking lot on N. School St in Kalihi
talkStory: Founded in 1946 by Helen 'Helena' Chock in a Kalihi strip mall, Helena's won the James Beard America's Classics Award in 2000 — the only traditional Hawaiian restaurant ever honored and a landmark moment for indigenous cuisine recognition. Now run by her grandson Craig Katsuyoshi, the pipikaula short ribs (Hawaiian-style dried beef) are the signature you cannot skip. Cash only, limited hours (Tue-Fri), and the line moves slow — but this is sacred-level Hawaiian food. Eat poi with two fingers, never one, never a fork.
